[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Annotation: @Upsertable(history = true)] During a network partition, can an unprivileged user cause the history-writing logic to create a `TokenHistory` entry with a `timestampRange` that has an open upper bound (infinity) on one node, while the current `Token` record on another node has already advanced past that timestamp, causing the history table to contain a phantom current-state entry that corrupts all historical queries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: freezeStatus, Field: timestampRange] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where a freeze transaction creates a `TokenHistory` entry on one node but the corresponding `Token` record update is lost, causing the history table to show a freeze event that is not reflected in the current state, permanently desynchronizing history and current state for `freezeStatus`?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: pauseStatus, Field: timestampRange] During a network partition, can an unprivileged user cause a pause transaction to be written to `TokenHistory` on one node with a `timestampRange` that starts before the partition began, causing historical queries to incorrectly show the token as paused during a period when it was actually active, corrupting audit trails?
